teachingPoint: Privacy enforcement must be synchronous inline — queue-driven T81 produces stale check results and adds queue round-trip latency to a synchronous path. T73/T76/T78 inject T81 as @Injectable() and call it synchronously before any write or emit.
positiveExample: T81 injected via constructor, called synchronously: this.privacyGatekeeper.check() returns result before storeDocument or enqueue.
negativeExample: T73 emits 'privacy.check.requested' event and awaits T81 result via queue before proceeding.
discriminatingConstraint: T81 is a constructor-injected @Injectable(). T73 calls this.privacyGatekeeper.check() synchronously. There is no code path where T73 emits a queue message to request a privacy check.
keywords: privacy gate inline synchronous no queue T81 PrivacyGatekeeper injectable

teachingPoint: Bidirectional social graph edges must be written in one atomic transaction. Two separate writes create a partial-write window: if the process crashes between A→B and B→A, User A sees User B in their connection list but User B does not see User A. No error is surfaced.
positiveExample: Both A→B and B→A adjacency entries in ONE ORM transaction. Either both succeed or neither succeeds.
negativeExample: Two separate storeDocument calls: store A→B edge, then store B→A edge.
discriminatingConstraint: Both adjacency entries are in a single ORM transaction. There is no code path where A→B is written without B→A being written atomically in the same operation.
keywords: bidirectional atomic graph write social connection ORM transaction edges

teachingPoint: T77 is a scoring node, not a gate node. score=0 means lowest personalization relevance — the item still passes through to T78. T78 is responsible for delivery thresholds and volume rules. When T77 filters zero-score items, weak connections produce empty feeds silently.
positiveExample: All items pass through T77 regardless of score. DataProcessResult.success({ item, score }) for every input. score=0 exits T77 identically to score=1.
negativeExample: Items with score <= minimum_relevance_threshold are dropped in T77 before T78 receives them.
discriminatingConstraint: T77 produces a score value for every input item. There is no code path that drops, skips, or defers an item based on its score value.
keywords: score zero passthrough feed scoring T77 FeedScorer no filter no threshold

teachingPoint: Privacy settings can change between T76 feed generation and T78 feed delivery. T78 must call T81 independently regardless of T76's result — the two-phase gate is mandatory. Trusting T76's stale result delivers blocked content after the user updates their privacy.
positiveExample: T78 calls this.privacyGatekeeper.check() unconditionally. No reference to T76 privacy outcome in T78.
negativeExample: T78 reads feedItem.privacyCheckPassedAtGeneration flag set by T76 and skips T81 if true.
discriminatingConstraint: T78 calls this.privacyGatekeeper.check() unconditionally. There is no code path where T78 reads a T76-generated privacy field, flag, or cached result as a substitute for calling T81 directly.
keywords: two phase privacy gate independent T78 T76 T81 stale check delivery generation

teachingPoint: Delta increments are not idempotent. Queue message redelivery causes T80 to process the same ConnectionEstablished event twice, incrementing count twice. Full recompute from graph intersection produces the same count regardless of retry count — idempotency is achieved through the algorithm.
positiveExample: Read adjacency lists for both users, intersect, count from scratch on every invocation. Same inputs always produce same count.
negativeExample: Read stored mutualCount, add 1 for new connection.
discriminatingConstraint: T80 reads adjacency lists for both users and intersects them. There is no code path that reads a stored mutualCount and increments or decrements it.
keywords: full recompute mutual count T80 no delta idempotent graph intersection

teachingPoint: SocialConnectionEstablished gates on T75 graph write completion only. Feed integration (T76-T78) involves AI generation (up to 10s), scoring, and feed writes. Waiting for all three means the user waits 10+ seconds for connection confirmation. T75 completes in <1s. Feed integration is time-decoupled and emits FeedIntegrationCompleted independently.
positiveExample: SocialConnectionEstablished emitted when T75 completes bidirectional graph write. T76-T78 async, time-decoupled.
negativeExample: SocialConnectionEstablished waits for T78 FeedDelivered before firing.
discriminatingConstraint: SocialConnectionEstablished is emitted by T75 when graph write completes. There is no code path where SocialConnectionEstablished waits for T76, T77, or T78.
keywords: SocialConnectionEstablished completion event T75 gate time decoupled feed integration
